qa.user_verbatim_complaint: commands/spec-update.md continuation-spec mode never writes the <!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} --> marker required by commands/commit.md Step 7 Stage 3. Fix the producer.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: The instruction was added to commands/spec-update.md lines 54-62 within ## Continuation-spec mode section, directing the agent to write the marker before the first new cycle block. The instruction addresses the root cause: the missing producer-side instruction that commit.md Stage 3 requires.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: grep -n 'spec-continuation-of' commands/spec-update.md returns line 55, confirmed inside ## Continuation-spec mode (line 31) before ## Temp-note mode (line 93)

qa.overall_assessment: ITERATION 2 (stubs implemented). All 5 generated tests pass (5 passed, 0 failed, 0.02s). Zero TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels remain in any test file. AC4 static grep independently confirmed: one occurrence of 'spec-continuation-of' at line 55 of commands/spec-update.md, within ## Continuation-spec mode section (line 31) before ## Temp-note mode (line 93). Provenance confirmed via git diff against baseline SHA f79fba1c — commands/spec-update.md is modified (+12/-2 lines). Instruction text is functionally correct: marker template, idempotency, mode restriction, and no-literal-placeholder prohibition all present. Codex adversarial consultation found no blocking issues — three out_of_scope hardening suggestions recorded in out_of_scope_observations.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause was missing producer instruction in spec-update.md. Fix adds a 10-line prose paragraph (lines 54-62) in ## Continuation-spec mode directing the agent to write the marker before the first new cycle block. The paragraph is in the correct procedural position. commit.md:164 greps for the marker; spec-update.md now instructs producing it with the identical resolved value.

This change instructs an AI agent; it is not a web page, API, or user-interactive component.

ac4_static_verification.description: AC4 is the only statically verifiable AC. Verified directly.
ac4_static_verification.evidence: Single occurrence at line 55, between line 31 (## Continuation-spec mode) and line 93 (## Temp-note mode). awk filter of Temp-note mode section returns no spec-continuation-of matches.

idempotency_check_present.evidence: Line 59: 'If it is already present, do not write a second copy.'

scope_restriction_to_continuation_mode.evidence: Lines 60-61: 'This marker is written exactly once per (task-id, spec file) pair and must appear only in this continuation-spec mode, never in temp-note mode.'

no_literal_placeholder_prohibition.evidence: Lines 56-57: '(substituting the actual task-id value — never a literal `${TASK_ID}` or `<task-id>` placeholder)'

placement_before_section_content.evidence: Line 57-58: 'write it as the very first line of the new cycle block, before any section headings'. Instruction block (lines 54-62) precedes 'Populate the 8-section template' (line 63).

marker_format_correct.evidence: Line 55 shows: '<!-- spec-continuation-of: <resolved-task-id> -->' with explicit note that <resolved-task-id> is to be substituted with the actual value. Runtime output matches commit.md:164 grep target when resolved.

notation_drift_analysis.codex_assessment: NOT a blocking issue. Both forms are documentation-level variable notation; both resolve to the same wire string at runtime. spec-update.md:56-57 explicitly prohibits writing placeholders.
notation_drift_analysis.qa_assessment: Notation drift is a documentation quality concern (spec_text_vs_execution_drift dimension) but does NOT cause runtime mismatch. An agent reading spec-update.md would produce the resolved marker identical to what commit.md Stage 3 greps for. Not raising as a blocking finding.
notation_drift_analysis.codex_proposed_hardening: Rewrite to include concrete example and forbid all placeholder forms including <resolved-task-id> itself. OBSERVATION_ONLY — no PROPOSED_FIX that is blocking.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex: placeholder prohibition omits '<resolved-task-id>' itself. The instruction template uses '<resolved-task-id>' as the illustrative form but only prohibits '${TASK_ID}' and '<task-id>' as literals. Hardening: add '<resolved-task-id>' to the prohibition list and include a concrete example like '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260524-125300-C -->'. Not a blocking issue — a competent agent follows the substitution intent; adding explicit prohibition reduces risk for edge-case agents.

Codex: AC5 verification recipe uses 'grep -c' which counts matching lines, not exact occurrences. Two markers on one line would pass falsely. Stronger recipe: grep -oF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: T -->' <spec_path> | wc -l. BA should update AC5 check command in a future spec revision (spec_text_vs_execution_drift candidate).

Codex: AC2 test uses disjunction ('write it as the very' OR 'first line of the new cycle block') which could pass even if one substring drifts. Hardening: assert the complete normalized phrase 'write it as the very first line of the new cycle block'. Not a current defect — both substrings are present in spec-update.md.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 5 issues with PROPOSED_FIX and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY: (1) static tests don't prove live production; (2) prohibition omits '<resolved-task-id>'; (3) AC5 idempotency indirect; (4) AC2 disjunction could drift; (5) scoped git status needed; (6) OBSERVATION_ONLY: AC4 evidence sufficient. Codex independently ran pytest and confirmed 5 passed in 0.02s.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 5 incorporated: scoped git diff --stat executed and documented in provenance_verification. Findings 1/3/4: classified out_of_scope — task scope is instruction-text-only; ACs were designed as static checks by BA (confirmed by AC type=data and BA qa_notes). Finding 2: classified out_of_scope hardening; recorded in out_of_scope_observations. All 3 hardening suggestions routed to out_of_scope_observations for BA ledger. No changes to verdict — zero blocking findings from codex.
